payload.hypothesis: Start with a balanced configuration that enables bundle generation and uses CP-SAT for optimal assignment, with moderate exploration settings to handle the rider-constrained benchmark profile.
Start with a balanced configuration that enables bundle generation and uses CP-SAT for optimal assignment, with moderate exploration settings to handle the rider-constrained benchmark profile.

payload.summary: Initial baseline achieved 24.3 completed orders with moderate bundle generation and CP-SAT optimization.
payload.keep_reason: Establishes solid baseline performance with balanced configuration for rider-constrained scenario.
risks: Bundle pool size (6) may limit exploration of alternative bundling options
Max bundle size of 2 may miss opportunities for larger efficient bundles
LNS destroy fraction (0.2) might be too conservative for effective exploration
next_focus: Test larger bundle pool sizes to increase solution diversity
Experiment with max_bundle_size=3 to capture potential triple-order efficiencies
Increase LNS destroy fraction to 0.3-0.4 for more aggressive neighborhood search
avoid_patterns: Making simultaneous changes to multiple exploration parameters
Ignoring rider capacity constraints when increasing bundle sizes

payload.hypothesis: Increase bundle candidate pool size to 8 and max bundle size to 3 to explore more bundling opportunities in rider-constrained scenarios, while keeping other parameters stable to isolate the impact of expanded bundle exploration.

Increase bundle candidate pool size to 8 and max bundle size to 3 to explore more bundling opportunities in rider-constrained scenarios, while keeping other parameters stable to isolate the impact of expanded bundle exploration.

payload.summary: Expanding bundle candidate pool to 8 and max bundle size to 3 reduced completed orders by 2.4% while slightly lowering costs, indicating the search became less effective at finding high-value assignments.
payload.keep_reason: This configuration should be discarded as it regressed the primary objective of maximizing completed orders.
risks: Larger bundle search space may overwhelm solver within time budget
Increased bundle complexity may lead to suboptimal rider assignments
Higher computational cost without corresponding quality improvement
next_focus: Test smaller bundle pool size increments (e.g., 4-6)
Evaluate bundle quality metrics separately from assignment quality
Consider tightening bundle distance threshold to maintain quality
Monitor solver convergence patterns with expanded search space
avoid_patterns: Simultaneously increasing both pool size and max bundle size
Expanding search space without quality controls
Ignoring time budget constraints when adding complexity

payload.hypothesis: Test a more aggressive LNS exploration strategy with increased destroy fraction (0.3) while maintaining the successful bundle configuration from the incumbent. This should help escape local optima without expanding the bundle search space that proved detrimental in the previous experiment.

Test a more aggressive LNS exploration strategy with increased destroy fraction (0.3) while maintaining the successful bundle configuration from the incumbent. This should help escape local optima without expanding the bundle search space that proved detrimental in the previous experiment.

payload.summary: Aggressive LNS exploration with 0.3 destroy fraction maintained solution quality but significantly increased solve time without improving objective.
payload.keep_reason: Solution quality matches incumbent but solve time increased 67% with no benefit.
risks: LNS destroy fraction too aggressive causing excessive reconstruction work
Solve time degradation may scale poorly with larger instances
Increased iterations not compensating for larger destroy fraction
next_focus: Reduce LNS destroy fraction to 0.15-0.2 range
Test time-bounded LNS iterations instead of fixed count
Monitor solve time vs solution quality tradeoff more closely
avoid_patterns: Increasing destroy fraction without time budget adjustment
Fixed iteration counts ignoring time per iteration
Aggressive LNS without evidence of local optima escape need
